channel.title: Arcbotix
channel.description: Practical robotics engineering guides and niche technical deep-dives

item.title: Monte Carlo Localization (Particle Filter) for Mobile Robots: A Worked Numeric Example
item.description: Monte Carlo localization explained with real numbers: a small particle set carried through motion prediction, landmark-range weighting, and low-variance resampling.

item.title: Torque Control for Robot Arm Joints: From Motor Current to Commanded Torque
item.description: Learn robot joint torque control from current sensing: the Kt torque constant, a cascaded current/velocity/position loop, and a worked PI current-loop tuning example.

item.title: Cascaded PID Control for Robot Joints: A Worked Bandwidth-Separation Example
item.description: How to tune a cascaded PID control loop (current, velocity, position) for a robot joint with a worked bandwidth-separation example and real PI gain numbers.

item.title: S-Curve Trajectory for a Robot Joint: A Worked Jerk-Limited Motion Profile
item.description: Compute a jerk-limited S-curve trajectory for a robot joint step by step: the seven phases, real J/A/V numbers, phase durations, and the short-move check.

item.title: Trajectory Planning for Robotic Arms: From Waypoints to Smooth Motion
item.description: Trajectory planning for robotic arms explained: joint-space vs Cartesian trajectories, trapezoidal and S-curve profiles, and how to avoid jerky motion.

item.title: Bearing Selection for Robot Arm Joints: A Worked Moment Load Example
item.description: Worked example of bearing selection for robot arm joints: compute the moment load, equivalent load on a crossed roller, and when two ball bearings suffice.

item.title: Motor and Gearbox Sizing for a Robot Arm Joint: A Worked Torque Calculation
item.description: Motor and gearbox sizing for a robot arm joint explained with a full worked example: load torque, safety factor, gear ratio choice, and a continuous-torque check.

item.title: PID Anti-Windup for Robot Joints: A Worked Integrator Windup Example
item.description: A worked PID anti-windup example for robot joints: how integrator windup causes overshoot at the torque limit, with clamping and back-calculation code.

item.title: PID Controller Tuning for Robotic Arms: A Practical Guide
item.description: Learn how PID controller tuning works for robotic arm joints, with formulas, tuning methods, and practical fixes for overshoot, oscillation, and steady-state error.

item.title: Mecanum Wheel Kinematics: A Worked Example from Velocity Command to Wheel Speeds
item.description: Mecanum wheel kinematics with real numbers: the inverse kinematics equations, wheel speeds in rad/s and RPM, normalization, and why strafing drifts.

item.title: Differential Drive Robot Kinematics and Wheel Odometry: A Worked Example
item.description: Differential drive robot kinematics explained with the full odometry derivation, real encoder numbers, and a worked example showing how drift accumulates.

item.title: Robot Joint Velocity Estimation from Encoder Counts: Why Finite Differences Get Noisy
item.description: Robot joint velocity estimation from encoder counts: worked quantization noise math, windowed differences, 1/T timing, and a tracking loop observer.

item.title: Absolute vs Incremental Encoders for Robot Joints: How to Size the Resolution You Actually Need
item.description: Absolute vs incremental encoder for robot joints, explained with a worked example: converting a target joint accuracy into encoder bits, PPR, and gear ratio.

item.title: Sliding Mode Control for Robot Arm Joints: A Worked Numeric Example
item.description: Sliding mode control for a robot arm joint explained with real numbers: sizing the sliding surface, the switching gain, and the boundary layer that kills chattering.

item.title: Ball Screw vs Lead Screw for a Robot Linear Actuator: A Worked Torque Sizing Example
item.description: Ball screw vs lead screw for a robot linear actuator, with a full worked torque sizing example for a SCARA Z-axis lift, including holding torque and backdrive behavior.

item.title: Tool Center Point (TCP) Calibration for Robot Arms: A Worked Pivot Calibration Example
item.description: TCP calibration for robot arms explained with an actual worked pivot-calibration example: the least-squares math behind the offset, not just the procedure.

item.title: RS-485/Modbus for Robot Joint Networks: Wiring, Addressing, and Polling-Rate Budget
item.description: RS-485/Modbus robot joint network wiring, addressing, and the frame-time math that determines how fast you can actually poll N daisy-chained joint drivers.

item.title: EtherCAT for Robot Joint Networks: How the Cycle-Time Budget Actually Works
item.description: EtherCAT for robot joint networks explained with a worked cycle-time budget: frame transmission time, per-slave forwarding delay, and cable propagation.

item.title: Zero Moment Point for Legged Robot Balance: A Worked Numeric Example
item.description: Learn the zero moment point (ZMP) stability criterion for legged robots with a worked numeric example: computing ZMP location and checking it against the support polygon.

item.title: Series Elastic Actuator Design: Sizing the Spring Stiffness for a Robot Joint
item.description: A worked example for sizing series elastic actuator spring stiffness: bandwidth, peak torque, and deflection-to-torque calibration for a robot joint.
